title: A multiple winding design for single or polyphase electric motors with a cage type rotor
abstract: An electric motor, with a cage type rotor, of single or polyphase design having a multiple winding design in a single stator core. Where the primary winding and secondary winding having a magnetically and electric circuit configured alignment, that is a 0 difference in the respective magnetic alignments or the electric circuitry alignments. Yet completely separate and isolated from one another, electrically and physically. Wherein the prime operative function is configured in a series resonant and a parallel resonant circuit, using capacitors to produce a phase shift in the secondary winding. With the ability to be reconfigured to a secondary operative function, identical to the original winding and in all of its original operative designed functions.
claims:
Claims An electric motor, with a cage type rotor, of single or polyphase design comprising a multiple winding design in a single stator core; wherein the multiple winding design comprises a primary winding and secondary winding; wherein the primary winding and secondary winding comprises a magnetically and electric circuit configured alignment, wherein said alignment is a 0Â° difference in the respective magnetic alignments or the electric circuitry alignments; wherein the primary winding and secondary winding are separate and isolated from one another, electrically and physically; wherein a prime operative function is configured in a series resonant and a parallel resonant circuit, using a p l u r al ity o f capacitors to produce a phase shift in the secondary winding; and wherein the primary winding and secondary winding comprises the structural arrangement to be reconfigured to a secondary operative function identical to an original winding and in all of its original operative designed functions. 

 The electric motor of claim 1 comprising: a cage type rotor; a stator core having a plurality of slots; a primary winding having a plurality of coils that corresponds to the original windings plurality of coils; a secondary winding having a plurality of coils that corresponds to the primary winding plurality of coils; wherein the primary winding configured layout in the stator core corresponds to the original winding configured layout in the stator core; with the secondary winding configured layout corresponding to that of the primary winding configured layout in the stator core; or when it is a single phase electric motor there shall be no configured change of or to the start/aux winding or to the configured circuitry layout relationship of it to the original winding; then with the primary winding stator core leads, along with the corresponding secondary winding stator core leads, and with capacitors, can be manually connected, or through switching devices, so as to be configured in the primary mode of operation; or the primary stator core leads with the corresponding secondary winding stator core leads, can be manually connected, or through switching devices, so as to be configured in the secondary mode of operation, the originally designed mode of operation. 3. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the original winding plurality of coils of claim 2 may be recalculated by use of a redesign method of the original winding for a new coil plurality of the original winding. 4. The electric motor of claim 3, wherein the use of redesign method comprises the primary winding plurality of coils corresponds and is mandated to correspond to that of the new plurality of coils for the original winding. 5. The electric motor of claim 4, wherein the use of redesign method comprises the secondary winding plurality of coils corresponding and mandated to correspond to that of the new plurality of coils of the primary winding . 6. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the primary winding turns are one in the same as to that of the original winding turns, or if claim 3 redesign method, then use of the new plurality of coils turns of the redesigned original winding is mandated. 7. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the secondary winding turns shall correspond to that of the primary winding turns of claim 6. 8. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the original winding circuitry layout be as such, so as to correspond to the applied operational voltage that the electric motor shall be operate on, for motors that have multiple voltage ratings. 9. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the primary winding circuitry layout shall correspond to the original winding circuitry layout of claim 8. 10. The electric motor of claim 1, further comprising the primary winding circuitry layout of claim 9, for if it is a polyphase electric motor, wherein the primary winding circuitry of claim 8 can be of an open circuit connection, that being all winding phases are individually separate and unto their own phase circuitry, with no connecting circuits between them. 11. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the secondary winding circuitry shall correspond to the primary winding circuitry of claim 10, where all winding phases are individually separate and unto their own phase circuitry,; with no connecting circuits between them. 12. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the original winding chord factor or factors being set as per the poling, by the plurality of slots, and the original winding plurality of coils of claim 2. Wherein though if redesign claim 3 in place, then the new chord factor or factors for the original winding of the redesign method claim 3 is mandated. 13. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the primary winding chord factor or factors shall correspond to that of the original winding original chord factor or factors, or whereas if claim 3 redesign method in place, then the new chord factor or factors of the redesign method claim 3 for the original winding is mandated. 14. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the original winding poling is set as per its plurality of slots, plurality of coils, and its circuitry layout of claim 2, the primary winding poling shall be one in the same as the original winding, with the secondary winding being one in the same as the primary winding poling with zero degree difference between the two for magnetic and circuitry alignment. 15. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the original winding wire size being of American wire gauge (AWG), Metric wire gauge, or British Standard wire gauge (SWG). 16. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the primary winding wire size as being averaged at 0.79 to the original sizing in claim 15. 17. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the secondary winding wire size as being averaged at 0.22 to the original sizing in claim 15. 18. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein the capacitors of claim 2, are of non-electrolytic, alternating current, non-polarized type capacitors, comprise a series resonant circuit with the secondary winding, and said series resonant circuit joined with said primary winding to comprise a parallel resonant circuit. 19. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein said primary mode of operation claim 2, where the primary winding lead end circuitry connections claim 9, with further comprising of claim 10, with secondary winding lead end circuitry connections claim 11, with capacitors claim 18, to configure, to comprise a series resonant and a parallel resonant circuit configuration, reverse connected, magnetically aligned. 20. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein said secondary mode of operation of claim 2, where the primary winding lead end circuitry connections claim 9, with further comprising of claim 10, with secondary winding lead end circuitry connections claim 11, to 

 comprise a magnetically aligned winding identical to the original winding of said electric motor. 21. The electric motor of claim 1, wherein if driven above its synchronous rpm can be used as an induction generator no matter which mode of operational connection it is configured in.
